outcome_text: The functionality and behavior of the AI system and its components—as identified in the MAP function—are monitored when in production.
scoring_text: Skill in production AI system monitoring, including implementing observability for AI system functionality and behavior, tracking component-level performance against MAP-phase specifications, and maintaining production monitoring aligned with pre-deployment risk assessments.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in production AI system monitoring, including implementing observability for AI system functionality and behavior, tracking component-level performance against MAP-phase specifications, and maintaining production monitoring aligned with pre-deployment risk assessments.
reference_document_element_description: Operate continuous evaluation processes for deployed AI systems, monitoring for performance degradation, behavioral drift, and emerging failure modes.
comments: NIST AI RMF MS-2.4 (MEASURE) equal WIDAI RM domain. RM-T-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.707, cosine: 0.712). Secondary: DG-T-007 (DG, cosine: 0.700), SP-T-009 (SP, cosine: 0.660).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.1/10 (raw 0.707). Shared vocabulary: [ai, monitoring, performance]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [against, aligned, assessments, behavior, component, deployment] vs WIDAI terms [behavioral, continuous, degradation, deployed, drift, emerging] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.888 (P=0.881, R=0.896). Balanced P/R — the 30-word competency implication and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.3%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.712 ≈ STS 0.707 (gap 0.006). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, monitoring, performance] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.5%. Shared tokens: [ai, monitoring, performance]. NIST AI RMF-only: [against, aligned, assessments, behavior, component, deployment]. WIDAI-only: [behavioral, continuous, degradation, deployed, drift, emerging].
